frontmatter.pricing_logic: CUT VINYL BASELINE. AI consensus was $38-42 at qty 20. Nick priced at $35 as Relationship Concession on ~$140K annual account. Validates within Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile at ~$13.67/sq ft (concession-phase; AI consensus band was $14.84-$16.41/sq ft).
frontmatter.notes: First item quoted to Elliott. Sean ordered FA next morning without pushback. 3M 180mC-53 Cardinal Red on 24\" roll.

sections.item_overview:
- **Part Number:** 1205720
- **Description:** "E190" Cardinal Red Model Designation
- **Type:** Vinyl cut lettering (no printing)
- **Content:** Large "E190" model designation text in Cardinal Red. Applied to crane body for model identification.
- **Dimensions:** 33-9/16" W × 11" H (~2.56 sq ft)
- **Tolerances:** ±1/16" (assumed account default)
- **DWG Date:** Original — no revision history
- **Status:** FA Accepted

---
sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** 3M Controltac 180mC-53 Cardinal Red
- **Film Type:** 2-mil calendered vinyl
- **Adhesive:** Controltac repositionable pressure-sensitive
- **Roll Size:** 24" × 10yd = ~$153.60/roll ($15.36/yard)
- **Application Tape:** TransferRite Ultra 582U, 30" × 100yd = $135.06 ($0.18/sq ft)
- **Laminate:** N/A

---
sections.nesting:
- **Nesting on 24" roll:** 2 labels per row (11" + 11" = 22", fits 24" roll width). Linear footage per row: ~33.5625" (~0.93 yards).
- **Vinyl cost per label:** ~$6.55 (2.56 sq ft × $2.56/sq ft for Cardinal Red)
- **Application tape cost:** ~$0.46/label (2.56 sq ft × $0.18/sq ft)
- **Total material cost per label:** ~$7.01

---
sections.production:
1. File prep — import DWG/PDF, set up cut file
2. Cut — Roland SG3-300 on 24" stock
3. Weed — remove excess vinyl around cut characters
4. Mask — apply TransferRite 582U application tape
5. Inspect and package

- **Lamination Passes:** 0
- **Cut Runs:** 1
- **Constraints:** None — simple cut/weed/mask process

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
- **Methodology:** Validated across ~12 AI model responses. Consensus range was $38-42 per label at qty 20.
- **Benchmark Item:** None — this establishes the cut vinyl baseline.
- **Override:** Nick priced at $35, below AI consensus, as a deliberate Relationship Concession on a ~$140K annual account. First item quoted — set the tone for the relationship.
- **Strategic:** $35 was competitive, professional, and signaled that Pro Label is serious about the program. Sean ordered FA next morning without pushback.
- **Profile Validation:** Establishes the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile at ~$13.67/sq ft (concession-phase). AI consensus would place the band at ~$14.84-$16.41/sq ft. Band expected to shift upward as relationship matures.

---
sections.notes:
- **CUT VINYL BASELINE.** First item quoted. Establishes the per-sq-ft rate for the entire cut vinyl category.
- **Relationship Concession.** $35 vs $38-42 consensus. Non-precedent-setting — future cut vinyl items should benchmark against the AI consensus band ($14.84-$16.41/sq ft), not the concession price ($13.67/sq ft). Override Type field flags this.
- **Sean's reaction:** Ordered FA next morning. No pushback. This set the trust level for the entire account.
- **Cardinal Red material:** Pre-stocked before first PO. $153.60/roll is the known cost.

---
sections.production_debrief: *No production debrief yet. Update after first real production run. Capture: actual nesting efficiency on 24" stock, weeding time for large lettering, whether 2-per-row layout worked as planned.*

frontmatter.pricing_logic: ROOT BENCHMARK. Validated against Printed + Laminated Pricing Profile — establishes the singles band at ~$15.43/sq ft at qty 20. All kit pricing traces through this item historically (1278930 = 1.5x, 1245130 = 5/3 parity).
frontmatter.notes: CRITICAL: Foundation of the entire kit pricing chain. If challenged, defend on value (ANSI compliance, durability, safety-critical accuracy), never cost-plus.

sections.item_overview:
- **Part Number:** 1230820
- **Description:** Load Chart Label, Model D105
- **Type:** Printed/Laminated Label (single)
- **Content:** Full-color lifting capacity load chart — rated capacity data tables, boom angle/radius diagrams, ANSI safety color coding, model-specific operating parameters. Safety-critical label.
- **Dimensions:** 15" W × 12.44" H (1.296 sq ft)
- **Tolerances:** ±1/16"
- **DWG Date:** Original — no revision history
- **Status:** FA Accepted

---
sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** Orajet 3951 cast vinyl, white, adhesive back (~2 mil)
- **Adhesive:** Permanent pressure-sensitive
- **Laminate:** 1-mil clear polyester (Mylar) — gloss finish
- **Laminator feed:** 12.44" dimension feeds through 13.5" laminator (1.06" clearance) — single pass
- **Application Tape:** N/A

---
sections.nesting:
- **Laminator Feed:** 12.44" feeds through 13.5" laminator — single pass
- **Print Layout:** One label per row on Roland SG3-300 (15" width within 28" max print width)
- **Material Cost Breakdown:**
  - Vinyl (Orajet 3951): 1.296 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft = ~$1.57
  - Laminate (1-mil polyester): ~$1.02
  - Ink (eco-solvent, medium-high coverage): ~$0.50
  - Waste/setup: ~$0.91
  - **Total: ~$4.00/label**

---
sections.production:
1. File prep — import PDF, set up print file with registration marks
2. Print — Roland SG3-300, full-color eco-solvent on Orajet 3951
3. Laminate — 1-mil clear polyester, ONE pass (12.44" feeds through 13.5")
4. Contour cut — Roland, rounded corners per spec
5. Weed — remove excess material
6. Inspect — print quality, laminate adhesion, cut accuracy, ANSI color fidelity
7. Package — flat, individually cut, stacked

- **Lamination Passes:** 1
- **Cut Runs:** 1
- **Constraints:** None — fits all equipment in single passes

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
- **Methodology:** Built from scratch — no prior benchmark existed.
- **Benchmark Item:** None — this IS the root benchmark.
- **AI Validation:** ~12 model responses confirmed the tier structure.
- **Strategic:** Priced to establish the relationship. $20 at qty 20 was set to be competitive and leave room for the kit multiplier structure.
- **Override:** None.
- **Profile Validation:** Establishes the Printed + Laminated singles band at ~$15.43/sq ft at qty 20.

---
sections.notes:
- **ROOT BENCHMARK.** The single most important price point on the account. 1278930 = 1.5x this. 1245130 = 5/3 parity off 1278930. If challenged, the entire kit structure is at risk.
- **Defense:** Value-based — ANSI compliance, outdoor durability, model-specific safety data accuracy, zero-drama fulfillment.
- **Per-sq-ft rate:** $15.43/sq ft at qty 20. Every future printed/laminated single will be compared against this.
- **Equipment fit:** Single passes on all equipment. Cleanest production item on the account.

---
sections.production_debrief: *No production debrief yet. Update after first real production run (not FA). Capture: actual material vs estimated, time, scrap, production issues, whether pricing assumptions held.*

frontmatter.pricing_logic: 5/3 parity with P/N 1278930. Per-label = $10.00 at qty 20, identical to 3-label kit. Validated across 30 model runs / 4 rounds. 1.8x was rejected — buyer psychology won over cost modeling. Validates within Printed + Laminated kit Pricing Profile band.
frontmatter.downstream_items: None yet. End of current chain.
frontmatter.notes: 2 lam passes due to 13.5\" laminator constraint. Parity boundary is tied to lamination passes (≤2), not label count.

sections.item_overview:
- **Part Number:** 1245130
- **Description:** Lifting Capacity Chart, Model E160
- **Type:** Printed full-color kit (5 labels: A through E)
- **Dimensions:** All 11.13" × 7.88" each (0.609 sq ft/label, ~3.045 sq ft total/kit)
- **Corners:** Rounded (R.13)
- **Tolerance:** ±.063"
- **Material Note (from drawing):** Cast 2-mil vinyl (5-7 yr), adhesive back, peel-off backing slit at middle, 1-mil clear Mylar overlay
- **DWG Date:** 05/24/19, Rev A (02/02/21 — changed 50,000 to 34,000)
- **Status:** Quoted May 2026

---
sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** Orajet 3951 cast vinyl, white, adhesive back (~2 mil)
- **Adhesive:** Permanent pressure-sensitive
- **Laminate:** 1-mil clear polyester (Mylar) overlaminate — gloss finish
- **Ink:** Eco-solvent, ANSI Z535.1 safety colors, Helvetica Bold lettering
- **Layout:** ANSI Z535.4 compliant

---
sections.nesting:
- **Laminator Feed:** 7.88" feeds through 13.5" laminator. Cannot fit all 5 in one pass — **2 lamination passes** (3 in pass 1, 2 in pass 2).
- **Print Layout:** All 5 fit in one print run on Roland SG3-300 (28" max). Three at 7.88" wide, two rotated to 11.13" wide.
- **Material Cost Breakdown (per kit):**
  - Vinyl (Orajet 3951): 5 × 0.609 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft = ~$3.68
  - Laminate (1-mil polyester): ~$1.15 (across 2 passes)
  - Ink (eco-solvent): 5 × ~$0.27 = ~$1.35
  - Waste/setup allowance: included
  - **Total material cost per kit: ~$6.03** (per frontmatter canonical value)
  - **Per-label material cost: ~$1.21**

---
sections.production:
1. File prep — import 5 engineering drawing pages, set up print layout
2. Print — 1 run, all 5 labels on Roland SG3-300
3. Laminate — **2 passes** (13.5" max width constraint)
4. Contour cut — **2 runs** (matching the 2 lam groups)
5. Weed, inspect, package as matched set (A through E)

- **Lamination Passes:** 2
- **Cut Runs:** 2
- **Key constraint:** Double lam pass is the sole production inefficiency. Wider laminator collapses to single-pass.

---
sections.notes:
- **Parity boundary is production-based.** Per-label parity applies at ≤2 lam passes, same dimensions, same material. Beyond 2 passes or mixed dimensions → cost-build.
- **Dollar profit per kit is the metric that matters.** $43.97 at qty 20 is excellent despite absorbing the double-pass.
- **Wider laminator upgrade** collapses to single-pass and improves margins without pricing change.

---
sections.production_debrief: *No production debrief yet. Update after first production run. Capture: actual material usage, time per kit, whether 2-pass lamination worked as planned, actual scrap on double-pass, and critically — does the 2-pass add meaningful time or is it negligible?*

frontmatter.pricing_logic: 1.5x multiplier off P/N 1230820 (root benchmark). Validated across 25 model runs / 6 rounds. Validates within Printed + Laminated kit Pricing Profile at $10.00/label ($16.42/sq ft). Frame as 'three separately finished safety labels supplied as one controlled matched set.
frontmatter.downstream_items: P/N 1245130 (5/3 parity). Bridge item between single-label benchmark and all future kits.
frontmatter.notes: All 3 labels fit in one lamination pass (3 × 7.88\" = 23.64\"). Most efficient kit configuration on current equipment.

sections.item_overview:
- **Part Number:** 1278930
- **Description:** CHRT-E190 V3, Integrated Platform Lifting Capacity Chart
- **Type:** Printed/Laminated Kit (3 labels: A, B, C)
- **Content:** Three separate lifting capacity charts for the E190 crane model. Each label contains rated capacity data, boom angle/radius diagrams, ANSI safety color coding, and model-specific operating parameters. Supplied as a matched set.
- **Dimensions:** All 11.13" × 7.88" each (0.609 sq ft/label, ~1.83 sq ft total/kit)
- **Corners:** Rounded, R.25 (4 corners)
- **Tolerances:** ±1/16" (assumed account default)
- **DWG Date:** V3 revision
- **Status:** FA Accepted

---
sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** Orajet 3951 cast vinyl, white, adhesive back (~2 mil)
- **Adhesive:** Permanent pressure-sensitive
- **Laminate:** 1-mil clear polyester (Mylar) overlaminate — gloss finish
- **Laminate Max Width:** 13.5" on current equipment — all 3 labels fit in one pass (7.88" feeds through)
- **Application Tape:** N/A
- **Ink:** Eco-solvent (Roland TR2), ANSI Z535.1 safety colors + full-color diagrams

---
sections.nesting:
- **Laminator Feed:** 7.88" feeds through 13.5" laminator. 3 × 7.88" = 23.64" — all 3 fit in ONE pass.
- **Print Layout:** All 3 labels in one print run on Roland SG3-300.
- **Material Cost Breakdown (per kit):**
  - Vinyl (Orajet 3951): 3 × 0.609 sq ft × $1.21/sq ft = ~$2.21
  - Laminate (1-mil polyester): ~$0.85 (3 labels across 13.5" pass)
  - Ink (eco-solvent): 3 × ~$0.27 = ~$0.81
  - Waste/setup allowance: included
  - **Total material cost per kit: ~$3.63** (per frontmatter canonical value)
  - **Per-label material cost: ~$1.21**

---
sections.production:
1. File prep — import 3 engineering drawing pages, tile on one layout with registration marks
2. Print — Roland SG3-300, full-color eco-solvent on Orajet 3951. All 3 in one run.
3. Laminate — 1-mil polyester. **ONE pass — all 3 fit.**
4. Contour cut — **ONE run** following registration marks. R.25 corners.
5. Weed — remove excess around all 3 labels
6. Inspect — print quality, lam adhesion, cut accuracy, label-to-label consistency
7. Package — collate A, B, C as matched set

- **Lamination Passes:** 1
- **Cut Runs:** 1
- **Constraints:** None — most efficient kit configuration on current equipment.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
- **Methodology:** Full multi-round AI model validation — 25 independent model runs across 6 rounds.
- **Benchmark Item:** P/N 1230820 (root benchmark, single label at $20 qty 20). This kit is 1.5x.
- **Multiplier Logic:** 1.5x consensus from Round 1. Reflects three separately finished labels produced, inspected, and packaged as a controlled matched set.
- **FA Pricing:** Debated $55-$90 across models. Consensus ~$75. Nick set $65 as trust-building move. Override: Relationship Concession on FA only. Production tiers are engine-validated.
- **200+ Tier:** Debated $17 vs $18. Destruction round tipped to $18 — permanent anchor risk + laminate upgrade absorption margin.
- **Profile Validation:** $10.00/label at qty 20 = $16.42/sq ft. Within the Printed + Laminated kit Pricing Profile band.

---
sections.notes:
- **BRIDGE ITEM.** Links root benchmark (1230820) to all future kit pricing. The 1.5x multiplier established here is the basis for 5/3 parity on 1245130.
- **Single-pass efficiency.** Most efficient kit configuration possible on current equipment. Any kit exceeding 13.5" lam capacity in one pass is less efficient.
- **Per-label parity.** $10.00/label at qty 20 matches the 5-label kit. Intentional and documented.
- **Defense:** Frame as process value (matched-set collation, multi-label inspection, controlled packaging), never as multiplier math.
- **Parity boundary:** Per-label parity applies when ≤2 lamination passes, same dimensions, same material. Beyond that, cost-build from scratch.

---
sections.production_debrief: *No production debrief yet. Update after first real production run (not FA). Capture: actual material usage vs estimated, time per kit, whether single-pass nesting worked as planned, scrap rate, whether pricing assumptions held.*

frontmatter.pricing_logic: Sq ft parity with P/N 1205720. Same ~2.56 sq ft, same process. Validates within Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile at ~$13.67/sq ft (concession-phase band). Material cost varies by roll width: $8.46 on 24\", $6.74 on 48\".
frontmatter.notes: LBL-ELLIOTT 8.38 WHT. 48\" roll dramatically better yield (5 across vs 2). Material cost drops from $8.46 to $6.74 on 48\" stock.

sections.item_overview:
- **Part Number:** 3017435
- **Description:** LBL-ELLIOTT 8.38 WHT — "ELLIOTT" brand lettering in white vinyl
- **Type:** Vinyl cut lettering (no printing)
- **Content:** Large "ELLIOTT" brand name. Applied to crane body for brand identification. Used across multiple models.
- **Dimensions:** 43.91" W × 8.38" H (~2.56 sq ft)
- **Tolerances:** ±1/16" (assumed account default)
- **DWG Date:** Original — no revision history
- **Status:** Quoted May 2026

---
sections.material_spec:
- **Substrate:** 3M Controltac 180mC-10 White
- **Film Type:** 2-mil calendered vinyl
- **Adhesive:** Controltac repositionable pressure-sensitive
- **Roll Options:**
  - 24" × 10yd = $131.16/roll ($13.116/yard, ~$2.19/sq ft)
  - 48" × 10yd = $257.44/roll ($25.744/yard, ~$2.15/sq ft)
- **Application Tape:** TransferRite Ultra 582U, 30" × 100yd = $135.06 ($0.18/sq ft)
- **Laminate:** N/A

---
sections.nesting:
### On 24" Roll
- 2 labels per row (8.38" + 8.38" = 16.76", fits 24" width)
- Linear footage per row: ~43.91" (~1.22 yards)
- Vinyl cost per label: ~$8.00 (1.22 yards × $13.116/yard ÷ 2)
- Tape: ~$0.46
- **Total: ~$8.46/label**

### On 48" Roll (dramatically better yield)
- 5 labels across (8.38" × 5 = 41.9", fits 48" width)
- Linear footage per row: ~43.91" (~1.22 yards)
- Vinyl cost per label: ~$6.28 (1.22 yards × $25.744/yard ÷ 5)
- Tape: ~$0.46
- **Total: ~$6.74/label**

### Recommendation
48" roll for any order of qty 10+. The per-label savings ($1.72) justify the larger roll purchase. 24" roll for small or one-off orders where stocking 48" isn't warranted.

---
sections.production:
1. File prep — import DWG/PDF, set up cut file
2. Cut — Roland SG3-300 on 24" stock, OR 54" plotter on 48" stock
3. Weed — remove excess vinyl around cut characters
4. Mask — apply TransferRite 582U application tape
5. Inspect and package

- **Lamination Passes:** 0
- **Cut Runs:** 1
- **Note:** 48" stock goes on the 54" plotter, not the Roland.

---
sections.pricing_derivation:
- **Methodology:** Sq ft parity with P/N 1205720. Same ~2.56 sq ft, same cut/weed/mask process, same material family. Identical tier structure.
- **Benchmark Item:** P/N 1205720 (sq ft parity)
- **AI Validation:** Not separately validated — direct parity with an accepted item at the same sq ft. No ambiguity.
- **Override:** None. Engine output matched the parity logic.
- **Profile Validation:** $35 / 2.56 sq ft = $13.67/sq ft at qty 20. Within the Cut Vinyl Pricing Profile band (concession-phase).

---
sections.notes:
- **Roll width is a margin lever.** 48" stock improves margin by 5 points at qty 20. Internal efficiency gain — don't pass savings to buyer.
- **Brand lettering across multiple models.** This item may have higher volume than model-specific labels since "ELLIOTT" goes on every unit.
- **54" plotter required for 48" stock.** Different equipment than the Roland SG3-300.
- **Quoted same day as 1245130.** Both items in a single email to Sean. No response yet.

---
sections.production_debrief: *No production debrief yet. Update after first production run. Capture: which roll width was used, actual nesting yield, weeding time for large brand lettering, whether 48" stock on 54" plotter worked cleanly.*
